title: User reports unexpected MFA prompts from another state

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] Unexpected MFA prompts from another state
servicenow_seed.description: User in NY received 6 MFA push notifications in 10 minutes. Sign-in attempts appear from a different state. User did not approve.

correct_steps: Verify caller via callback (the account may be compromised)
Tell the user NOT to approve any MFA prompt
Check sign-in logs: confirm location anomaly and times
Disable active sessions, force a password reset, require MFA re-registration
Escalate to Security Operations with timestamps, source IP, and user agent
rationale: Unexpected MFA prompts are an MFA-fatigue / push-bombing signal. Treat as a potential compromise and hand to SecOps with evidence.
hints: Never approve MFA on the user's behalf.
learning_objectives: Stop the active threat before opening a long ticket

tool_clues.sso: 6 sign-in attempts from IP in TX in last 10 min. User device is in NY.
tool_clues.ad: Account: Active. Last successful sign-in: 2 hours ago, from NY office.
